title: `except Exception` does not catch KeyboardInterrupt, SystemExit, or GeneratorExit
rule: KeyboardInterrupt, SystemExit, and GeneratorExit inherit from BaseException, not Exception, so `except Exception:` intentionally lets them propagate (Ctrl-C and sys.exit still work). The footgun is reaching for bare `except:` or `except BaseException:` "to be thorough" — that silently swallows Ctrl-C and exit signals, making retry loops and long tasks impossible to interrupt and breaking clean shutdown. Catch `Exception` for application errors; catch BaseException only if you re-raise (or use try/finally for cleanup).
triggers: Ctrl-C does not stop the program
KeyboardInterrupt not caught by except Exception
program won't terminate on keyboard interrupt
